claims.text: Alpenbrevet — Race Reference > Personal results, targets, and race strategy live in the events table ( propose_race_* / apply_race_* ).
--- ## Race Overview The Alpenbrevet is an annual alpine gran fondo based in Andermatt, Switzerland.
Athletes ride one of several route options crossing multiple high alpine passes in a single day.

Platinum route (the hardest, used by this athlete): - Distance: ~210–215 km (varies slightly by GPS and exact edition) - Elevation: 4,700–5,100m depending on direction and GPS accuracy - Category: ultra-endurance (10–15 hours depending on level) - Start: Andermatt (~1,440m altitude), early morning (~5:00–6:00 am local time) - Organised event with feed stations but largely self-supported pacing --- ## The Four Passes | Pass | Altitude | Key characteristic | |---|---|---| | Furka | 2,429m | Steep west face, gentler east (Realp) side | | Nufenen (Passo della Novena) | 2,478m | Highest paved pass in Switzerland.
